exports.summary: Tracks the feature-owned candidate pool and future draw eligibility.
Tracks the most recent committed candidate choice for the current feature session.
Update the weighted pool definition so the replacement reward is included.
Existing selection modal must be able to display the replacement reward payload.
Bind the replacement pool reward to the primary hero ability exposed by skill_provider_demo for grant-on-selection behavior.
Use the existing selection outcome realization point to trigger the ability grant when the replacement reward is confirmed.

consumes.summary: Capture one local trigger that opens the candidate selection flow.
Maintain a feature-owned candidate pool for future draws.
Draw 3 weighted candidates from the local pool.
Let the player choose exactly one candidate and commit it immediately.
Track selected eligibility removal and pool updates in same-feature session state.
Consumes a provider feature that can grant one primary hero ability.

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > Mapping Table v1
evidenceRefs.snippet:
| Source pattern / unit class | Typical realization | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `input.key_binding` | `ts` | Custom input capture and trigger orchestration are runtime concerns |
| `effect.dash` | `kv+ts` | Ability shell often fits static config; dash behavior usually needs r

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > KV + TS Hybrid Cases
evidenceRefs.snippet:
The following categories should generally bias toward `kv+ts`:

- Dota2 ability shell defined statically, but behavior implemented dynamically
- modifier registration via host-native naming/config with runtime logic in TS
- features where the host expects ability or modifier meta

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > Lua Cases (Narrow Scope — T125)
evidenceRefs.snippet:
The following may bias toward `lua`:

- ability + modifier patterns where Dota2 VScript is the preferred runtime medium
- short_time_buff-style mechanics with same-file ability and modifier layout
- cases where lua metadata schema has been explicitly defined

**Critical boundary*

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > Lua Cases (Narrow Scope — T125)
evidenceRefs.snippet:
- `ts`: TS authoring path -> TypeScriptToLua/toolchain -> Lua runtime
- `lua`: direct `.lua` authoring path -> Lua runtime

For lua-backed ability cases that still need a static shell, the current transitional realization shape is `kv+lua`.

This should be understood as:

- one r

evidenceRefs.title: Abilities API > 对 Rune Weaver 的直接价值
evidenceRefs.snippet:
- `input.key_binding` 触发后如何接入 ability 逻辑
- `effect.dash` 这类 server effect 最终落到 ability / unit 调用边界
- 后续技能绑定、冷却、施法约束时会继续依赖本类知识

headingPath: 对 Rune Weaver 的直接价值

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Events Knowledge > 5. 事件分类速查 > 按用途分类
evidenceRefs.snippet:
```
lifecycle          -> 游戏生命周期（开始、结束、状态切换）
player             -> 玩家相关（连接、断开、操作）
combat             -> 战斗相关（击杀、伤害、治疗）
ability            -> 技能相关（施法、冷却、效果）
ui                 -> 界面相关（点击、悬停、动画）
```

assumptions: The requested artifact is limited to the Lua ability file at the provided target path.
Ability KV registration, slotting, granting, and any future activation semantics are handled outside this artifact.
A no-op OnSpellStart is acceptable for an ability shell that must not produce in-feature gameplay outcomes.
No additional overrides are required in Lua for this shell-only stage.
This artifact is limited to the KV ability shell definition for the owned feature scope.
Using a hidden, passive, non-learnable behavior is the safest KV-only way to satisfy the invariant that the ability has no in-feature activation, UI, or gameplay outcome.
The corresponding Lua ScriptFile will exist as a separate owned artifact for the same module.

reviewReasons: Module 'mod_gameplay_backbone_0' was synthesized inside bundle 'gameplay_ability_mod_gameplay_backbone_0_1' because no reusable family/pattern implementation fully matched it.
Module has no templated pattern backing and remains exploratory.
